docs.title: About This Document
docs.text:
This documentation describes the Impinj Gen2X tag feature commands available through the IoT Connector interface on Zebra fixed RFID readers such as the FXR90, FX9600, and ATR7000.

Commands are available via both MQTT and REST API interfaces. Use the tabs throughout the tutorial to switch between the two.

docs.title: Supported Gen2X Features
docs.text: Feature Description Tag Protection Password-based tag locking that renders tags invisible without the correct 32-bit PIN Tag Visibility Enables the reader to see protected tags using a stored password Short Range Combines tag protection with reduced read range for physical security TagFocus Suppresses already-inventoried tags so the reader focuses on unread tags Tag Quieting Selectively silences individual tags by EPC FastID Returns both EPC and TID in a single inventory response

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Tutorial — Step-by-step walkthrough of all Gen2X commands
  • API Reference — Interactive OpenAPI documentation with schemas

Applicable Devices

Zebra Fixed RFID Readers: FXR90, FX9600, ATR7000

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
For the complete interactive API documentation with detailed schemas and response codes, see:

  • MQTT API Reference — RapiDoc interactive documentation
  • REST API Spec — OpenAPI 3.1 specification

docs.title: REST API Endpoints
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /cloud/impinjGen2X Retrieve current Gen2X configuration PUT /cloud/impinjGen2X Set Gen2X configuration

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
The REST API supports two authentication schemes:

Scheme Type Usage basicAuth HTTP Basic Used to call /cloud/localRestLogin to obtain a bearer token bearerAuth HTTP Bearer Used for all Gen2X API calls

docs.title: FastID
docs.text: Impinj FastID embeds the tag TID along with the EPC in every inventory response, eliminating the need for separate TID read operations and improving throughput.

docs.title: Enable FastID
Enable FastID with TID Selector
docs.text:
Optionally specify a TID word selector mask (TID[0] through TID[3]) to embed only a specific TID word in the response.

MQTTREST API 
{
  "command": "set_impinjGen2X",
  "command_id": "auto-generated",
  "payload": {
    "fastID": {
      "enabled": true,
      "tidSelector": "TID[0]"
    }
  }
}

{
  "fastID": {
    "enabled": true,
    "tidSelector": "TID[0]"
  }
}

docs.title: Disable FastID
Get Configuration
docs.text: Retrieves the current Impinj Gen2X feature configuration as last saved. Returns an empty object if no Gen2X configuration has been set yet. Only feature objects that were previously configured are included in the response.

docs.title: Request
Example Response
docs.text:
When all features are configured, the response includes all feature objects:

{
  "fastID": {
    "enabled": true,
    "tidSelector": "TID[0]"
  },
  "tagProtect": {
    "action": "enableTagProtection",
    "password": "77777777",
    "tagID": "e2801191a5030069073b426d",
    "enableShortRange": true
  },
  "tagFocus": {
    "enabled": true
  },
  "tagQuieting": {
    "basic": {
      "action": "quiet",
      "tagIDs": ["e2801191a5030069073b426d"]
    }
  }
}

When no configuration has been set:

{}

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This tutorial provides a walk-through of the steps to use Impinj Gen2X tag features on Zebra fixed RFID readers, including:

  • Protected Mode (TagProtect)
  • TagFocus
  • Tag Quieting
  • FastID

docs.title: Details
docs.text:
Impinj Gen2X is an enhancement to Gen2's radio and logical layers. Impinj Gen2X tags are required to leverage these features with Gen2X-enabled Zebra RFID Readers.

Users should verify whether their tags support these operations by referring to Impinj Gen2X tag specifications: http://www.impinj.com/Gen2X

docs.title: Command Structure
Authentication
docs.text:
If a bearer token was previously copied from the dashboard page, use it directly. If not, use Admin login credentials with Basic Auth, execute POST /cloud/localRestLogin, and copy the bearer token from the response.

Authorization: Bearer <your-token>

docs.title: Protected Mode
docs.text: Impinj TagProtect prevents unauthorized cloning and reading of tags using a password-based protection mechanism. It renders a tag invisible to an RFID reader — the tag becomes RF silent and does not respond to Gen2 commands unless the reader first provides the correct 32-bit password.

docs.title: Enable Tag Protection
docs.text:
Locks a specific tag so it becomes invisible to all readers unless the correct 32-bit password is provided during inventory.

MQTTREST API 
{
  "command": "set_impinjGen2X",
  "command_id": "auto-generated",
  "payload": {
    "tagProtect": {
      "action": "enableTagProtection",
      "password": "77777777",
      "tagID": "e2801191a5030069073b426d",
      "enableShortRange": true
    }
  }
}

PUT /cloud/impinjGen2X HTTP/1.1
Host: <reader-ip>
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer <token>

{
  "tagProtect": {
    "action": "enableTagProtection",
    "password": "77777777",
    "tagID": "e2801191a5030069073b426d",
    "enableShortRange": true
  }
}

cURL:

curl -X PUT https://<reader-ip>/cloud/impinjGen2X \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -d '{
    "tagProtect": {
      "action": "enableTagProtection",
      "password": "77777777",
      "tagID": "e2801191a5030069073b426d",
      "enableShortRange": true
    }
  }'

docs.title: Disable Tag Protection
docs.text:
Removes the Impinj TagProtect lock from a previously protected tag. The tag becomes visible to all readers again without any password requirement.

MQTTREST API 
{
  "command": "set_impinjGen2X",
  "command_id": "auto-generated",
  "payload": {
    "tagProtect": {
      "action": "disableTagProtection",
      "password": "77777777",
      "tagID": "e2801191a5030069073b426d"
    }
  }
}

{
  "tagProtect": {
    "action": "disableTagProtection",
    "password": "77777777",
    "tagID": "e2801191a5030069073b426d"
  }
}

Required fields

tagID is required for enableTagProtection and disableTagProtection. The password must be an 8-character hexadecimal string (32-bit).

docs.title: Setting it up
docs.text:
MQTTREST API

Connect to the reader's MQTT broker and subscribe to the response topic and data topic. Then publish commands to the command topic using the JSON structures shown in this tutorial.

All Gen2X configuration commands use "command": "set_impinjGen2X". After configuring the desired features, stop the radio and send a start command with "applyImpinjGen2X": true to apply the configuration.

All requests are sent to the reader's IP over HTTPS (default port 443). Configuration is done via PUT /cloud/impinjGen2X with a JSON body containing at least one feature object (fastID, tagProtect, tagFocus, or tagQuieting).

After configuring the desired features, use the start command with the applyImpinjGen2X flag to begin Gen2X operations.

Important

The radio must be stopped before applying Gen2X configuration. Starting with Gen2X active while the radio is already running will return an error.

docs.title: Short Range
docs.text: Short Range combines Impinj TagProtect with a reduced read range. The tag is protected AND restricted to respond only when the reader antenna is within close proximity, providing added physical security.

docs.title: Enable Short Range
Start with Gen2X
docs.text: After configuring the desired Gen2X features, stop the radio and then send the start command to apply all staged Gen2X settings and begin inventory.

docs.title: Request
Success Response
Command Summary
docs.text: Feature MQTT Command REST Method REST Endpoint Set Gen2X config set_impinjGen2X PUT /cloud/impinjGen2X Get Gen2X config get_impinjGen2X GET /cloud/impinjGen2X Start with Gen2X start (with applyImpinjGen2X: true) — —

docs.title: Tag Quieting
docs.text: Impinj TagQuieting selectively silences individual tags to reduce tag churn and improve read accuracy. This technique is particularly suitable for applications that require reading the same tag multiple times as quickly as possible without the overhead of reading extraneous tags.

docs.title: Quiet Tags
docs.text:
Silences specific tags so they do not participate in inventory rounds. Other tags in the field are unaffected.

MQTTREST API 
{
  "command": "set_impinjGen2X",
  "command_id": "auto-generated",
  "payload": {
    "tagQuieting": {
      "basic": {
        "action": "quiet",
        "tagIDs": [
          "e2801191a5030069073b426d",
          "e2801191a5030069073b426e"
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}

{
  "tagQuieting": {
    "basic": {
      "action": "quiet",
      "tagIDs": [
        "e2801191a5030069073b426d",
        "e2801191a5030069073b426e"
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Unquiet Tags
docs.text:
Restores previously silenced tags so they participate in inventory rounds again.

MQTTREST API 
{
  "command": "set_impinjGen2X",
  "command_id": "auto-generated",
  "payload": {
    "tagQuieting": {
      "basic": {
        "action": "unquiet",
        "tagIDs": [
          "e2801191a5030069073b426d"
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}

{
  "tagQuieting": {
    "basic": {
      "action": "unquiet",
      "tagIDs": [
        "e2801191a5030069073b426d"
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Tag Visibility
docs.text: Tag visibility controls whether the reader can see and inventory protected tags. No tagID is needed — only the password.

docs.title: Enable Tag Visibility
docs.text:
Tells the reader the password so it can see and inventory protected tags that would otherwise be invisible. Once enabled, the reader supplies the password automatically during each inventory round.

MQTTREST API 
{
  "command": "set_impinjGen2X",
  "command_id": "auto-generated",
  "payload": {
    "tagProtect": {
      "action": "enableTagVisibility",
      "password": "77777777"
    }
  }
}

{
  "tagProtect": {
    "action": "enableTagVisibility",
    "password": "77777777"
  }
}

docs.title: Disable Tag Visibility
docs.text:
Revokes the reader's stored password so it will no longer be able to see protected tags during inventory.

MQTTREST API 
{
  "command": "set_impinjGen2X",
  "command_id": "auto-generated",
  "payload": {
    "tagProtect": {
      "action": "disableTagVisibility",
      "password": "77777777"
    }
  }
}

{
  "tagProtect": {
    "action": "disableTagVisibility",
    "password": "77777777"
  }
}

No tagID needed

tagID must be omitted for enableTagVisibility and disableTagVisibility actions.

docs.title: TagFocus
docs.text: Impinj TagFocus instructs tags that have already been inventoried to remain unresponsive, enabling the reader to focus on other tags. By reducing tag chatter, TagFocus improves read rates and accuracy in dense tag environments. TagFocus targets tags inventoried in session S1.

docs.title: Enable TagFocus
Disable TagFocus
